url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation — Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified FA 2003 Sch 4ZA (5% additional-dwellings surcharge from 31 October 2024 per FA 2024 Autumn Budget) + Sch 9A (2% non-resident surcharge) + LTTADA 2017 Sch 1 + Sch 5 (Welsh LTT) + ITTOIA 2005 s.272A + s.863 + ss.620-628 + s.629 + ITA 2007 ss.850C-E + ss.863A-G + TCGA 1992 s.59 + s.59A + Sch 2 paras 6-12 (60-day CGT reporting) + FA 2003 Sch 15 paras 9-13 + para 17A + FA 2015 Sch 24 (Section 24 phasing) + FA 2024 (CGT 18%/24% from 30 October 2024) + LURA 2023 Part 4 + LGF(W)A 2024 + Housing (Wales) Act 2014 Part 1 + Housing Act 2004 Parts 2 + 3 + RHWA 2016 + RRA 2025 + LLPA 2000 + SI 2008/1911 + PA 1890 s.1 + s.2(1) + ECCTA 2023 Part 1 + Part 3 + TMA 1970 s.12B + s.12AA + s.9A + s.28A + CA 2006 s.388 + SI 2021/1076 + SI 2017/692 + DPA 2018 + IHTA 1984 s.216 + s.226 + FA 2004 Part 3 ss.57-77 + SI 2005/2045 + FA 2021 deemed-contractor reform + FA 2009 Sch 55 + Sch 56 + FA 2007 Sch 24 + SI 2019/892 + VATA 1994 s.55A anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §1 + §1.C + §1.I + §1.J + §1.O + §1.A + §4 + §5 + §11.A + §11.A.1 + §11.C cluster + §19 + §19.10 + §20 + §22 + §22.1 + §22.9/10 + §22.11 + §23 + §23.1-§23.3 + §25 + §25.4 + §25.12 + §26.9 + §27.1-§27.4 + §27.7 + §28 + §28.5 + §29 + §30 + §32 + §33 cluster + §35.2 locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed in M3-A-B1 + M3-A-B2 + M3-B-B1 + M3-C-B1 + M3-C-B2 D-2). Each extended brief landed at +178 to +201 lines of additional content (worked examples + 10-12 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). This pattern across A1/A2/B1/B2/C1/C2/C3 confirms that Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_CASE_LAW_INDEX_AS_HUB — pick 4 (case-laws) is the first case-law INDEX / HUB page in MW3. Page format is decision-grouped TABLE (not chronological list). Stage 2 brief surfaces the case-law table as the page's load-bearing artefact (Worked Example 1) and bakes the §16.35 STRICT GATE for case-citation verbatim verification into the universal-rules + do-not-write GREP. Stage 2 anti-templating notes explicitly identify chronological-list collapse as the typical competitor template + flag wrong citation form / year / court / holding wording as the typical failure mode. RUN session BAILII-re-verifies every citation at write.

D-4 STAGE2_CROSS_JURISDICTIONAL_INTEGRATION — pick 1 (btl-investment-plymouth-cardiff-landlord-hotspots) is the first England-Wales cross-jurisdictional page in the MW3 program. Stage 2 brief flagged a possible §20.W extension candidate (Welsh tenancy regime RHWA 2016 as named cluster lock parallel to §20 RRA 2025) — informational, not blocking; raised at Stage 2 review for Stage 1b consideration. The cross-jurisdictional integration anchors §1 (England SDLT) + §23.1-§23.3 (Welsh LTT) + §30 (council tax premium England LURA 2023 vs Welsh LGF(W)A 2024 split) + §26.9 (HMO + selective licensing — England wards vs Welsh mandatory all-landlord) + §20 (RRA 2025) + RHWA 2016 (Welsh tenancy) + §4 + §5 (uniform UK-wide). Counter-pattern to avoid: collapsing into a generic property-portal hotspot ranking without the tax-decision layer.

D-5 STAGE2_DECODER_META_PAGE — pick 2 (buy-to-let-market-sees-record-high-returns) is the first META / decoder page in MW3 — the page treats industry data releases (Paragon, Hamptons, BVA BDRC, Landbay) as INPUT not conclusion. Stage 2 brief built the methodology comparison table (gross yield vs net yield vs total return) as the page's central differentiator + the Section 24 worked example as the page's payoff (showing £3,000/year lower after-tax cash post-Section 24 on the £400,000 / 75% LTV / 5% interest reference scenario). Counter-pattern Stage 2 must avoid: yield-figure restatement piece OR picking a specific release as the page's spine. The page must outlive any particular release.

D-6 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-B-B1 + M3-C-B1 + M3-C-B2 patterns (~1.5-2 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-7 STAGE2_CLUSTER_LABEL_DRIFT_TRIPLE — picks 1 (btl-investment-plymouth-cardiff) + 3 (can-uk-landlords-still-use-llp-to-reduce-tax) + 5 (change-landlord-accountants) all carry the queue cluster label 'Property accountant for cities / regions' BUT each routes to a different and more appropriate category: pick 1 → portfolio-management (substantive BTL-investment-decision angle); pick 3 → incorporation-and-company-structures (substantive entity-choice angle); pick 5 → property-accountant-services (service-process angle). The pattern (3 of 6 picks in this batch carry the same mis-cluster label across three semantically distinct topics) suggests the queue cluster 'Property accountant for cities / regions' has been used as a default catch-all rather than a substantive category label. Stage 2 carries the cluster-drift flag forward in each brief's work log; recommend Stage 1b review the queue.jsonl labelling for the cluster as a whole. Parallel to the C10 bed-and-breakfasting cluster-drift noted in M3-C-B2 D-6; this batch surfaces a denser pattern.

D-8 STAGE2_F102_SAFE_INHERITED — F-102 (Booking.com / Airbnb cannib watch propagated to M3-C-B2 C11) does not apply to any pick in this batch (no OTA-host or short-term-let topics in M3-C-B3). No new F-numbers raised in this batch. F-100 (§36 professional-conduct HP-lock RESOLVED at Stage 1b) + F-101 (§37 share-exchange HP-lock candidate raised by M3-C-B1) + F-102 (M3-C-B2) remain the operative MW3 Bucket C flags through M3-C-B3 with no additions.

D-9 STAGE2_§20.W_WELSH_TENANCY_CANDIDATE — surfaced through pick 1 cross-jurisdictional integration. Welsh tenancy regime (Renting Homes (Wales) Act 2016) is currently carried by reference in the brief but is NOT a named HP cluster lock — only §20 RRA 2025 (England) is locked. As cross-jurisdictional England-Wales pages multiply (M3-C-B3 introduced the first one), a named §20.W cluster lock parallel to §20 may become operationally useful. Informational, not blocking. Raised for Stage 1b consideration.

D-10 STAGE2_§33_FULL_LOCK_PROPAGATED — §33 CIS architecture full lock at MW2 Stage 1b sign-off 2026-05-27 was tested through pick 6 (cis-deduction-by-contractors-in-the-uk). All five §33 do-not-write items + the FA 2021 deemed-contractor £3m threshold reform + the FA 2026 secondary Class 1 NIC 15% rate + the SI 2019/892 VAT reverse charge in force 1 March 2021 + the Ready Mixed Concrete [1968] employment-status three-element test all surfaced cleanly. §33 cluster lock fully operational for property-business CIS pages.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Stage 2 work log frame) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: pick 1 btl-investment-plymouth-cardiff (vs generic property-portal hotspot rankings tax-blind format; first England-Wales cross-jurisdictional pair on site); pick 2 buy-to-let-market-sees-record-high-returns (vs yield-figure restatement of any single industry release; META / decoder framing is differentiator); pick 3 can-uk-landlords-still-use-llp-to-reduce-tax (vs sales-led 'LLP saves tax' pre-FA-2014 template; four-and-three honest layered answer is differentiator; LLP-solves-Section-24 BANNED framing); pick 4 case-laws (vs chronological case-list template dominant in competitor field; decision-grouped TABLE format is differentiator; §16.35 STRICT GATE for case-citation verbatim verification); pick 5 change-landlord-accountants (vs '5 reasons to switch' firm-marketing template; operational rigour with named statute per step is differentiator); pick 6 cis-deduction-by-contractors-in-the-uk (vs generic construction-industry explainer template; property-business specificity for developers + deemed-contractor large landlord groups is differentiator; all five §33 do-not-write items BANNED framings). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; worked-example personas vary per pick (Mr Patel Plymouth landlord / Ms Singh Plymouth-Cardiff portfolio / Mr Patel mortgaged BTL / Ms Singh £250k second BTL / Mr Patel £400k disposal / Mr Patel + Mrs Patel family LLP / Mr Singh + Mrs Singh + Priya genuine family partnership / Patel Holdings Ltd founder-LtdCo hybrid LLP / Mr Patel single-member LLP SDLT-on-incorporation / table of 22 case-law decisions / Mr Patel multi-obligation switch / Ms Singh PCRT clearance / Mr Patel MTD-handover / Patel Holdings Ltd ECCTA cascade / Patel Developments Ltd mainstream contractor / Patel Holdings Ltd deemed contractor / Patel Developments Ltd + P&S Electrical deduction-rate ladder / Patel Developments Ltd VAT reverse charge parallel / Patel Developments Ltd + Mr Smith 18-month Ready Mixed Concrete risk — note: surnames recur across worked examples as anonymisation pool not unique-per-pick discipline per program convention).
